title: Search & Replace
intro: `/pattern` + `Enter` jumps to the next match, `n` repeats the search, `*` searches for the word under the cursor. `:%s/old/new/g` replaces every occurrence in the file.

exercises.instruction: Find the word `bug`: type `/bug` and press `Enter`.
exercises.initialCode:
line one here
nothing special
the bug is here
hints: `/` opens the search prompt; `Enter` jumps to the first match.

exercises.instruction: Search for `item` with `/item` + `Enter`, then press `n` to jump to the next match on the last line.
hints: `n` repeats the last search in the same direction.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on `count`. Jump to its next occurrence with `*`.
hints: `*` searches forward for the exact word under the cursor.

exercises.instruction: Replace every `cat` with `dog` in one command: type `:%s/cat/dog/g` and press `Enter`.
hints: `%` means the whole file, `g` means every match on each line.

exercises.instruction: Search for `beta` with `/beta` + `Enter`, then press `n` to reach the match on the last line.
exercises.initialCode:
alpha beta
gamma delta
beta again
hints: The first match is on line 1 â€” `n` jumps to the next one.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on `total`. Jump to its next occurrence with `*`.
hints: `*` searches forward for the exact word under the cursor.

exercises.instruction: Rename every `foo` to `bar` in one command: `:%s/foo/bar/g` + `Enter`.
hints: Without the trailing `g`, only the first match on each line is replaced.
